voice_observations.caption_text:
Seen in Every Sense:This International Women’s Day, Ounass brings together a panel of voices at Ounass Maison. Guided by Faiza Bouguessa, the conversation explores visibility, representation, and defining success on their own terms.
مرئية في كل حضور: في يوم المرأة العالمي، تقدّم أُناس جلسة حوارية نسائية في أُناس ميزون، تقودها أصوات ملهمة من النساء. تدير فايزة بوقصّة هذا الحوار الذي يستكشف مفهوم الظهور، وما يعنيه أن تكون المرأة مرئية، وكيف يعرّفن النجاح وفق معاييرهن الخاصة.
voice_observations.hook_text: Seen in Every Sense: This International Women’s Day
voice_observations.voiceover_text: I think she needs to find who she really is before the world tells her who she should be. Mariana Wehbet, Malak Abou-Kahoud, Najwa Bharfouch, Noor Bashir, and Maryam Sawadek and Brittany Small. Success isn't necessarily meaning going big and going to the masses as well. It's connecting one-on-one in a quiet room when people feel you and sense your authenticity and sense the practice or the method that we have developed specifically for women. And that's the biggest impact. What is visibility and what is success? And here we can say that success is alignment, being true to your values, your authentic self. Everybody wants to be strong and powerful and I don't know what. And strong, independent women. And I find the biggest strength in people is their vulnerability. At the end of the day, hard work pays off, but also having good values and principles also pays off. Because people really do see that. And just trusting your gut. Having the space for making mistakes and understanding that everything is an evolution and we're learning along the way. Because that's my purpose. I want to help them. Then I forced myself to be more in the limelight. Thank you, Eunice. Thank you. Thank you.
